name: MFI(10) >50 + Median
description: A trend-following strategy that combines a long-term price median filter with a short-period Money Flow Index. QQQ is used as the signal asset and TQQQ/BIL as execution vehicles. When both the median filter and MFI agree, the strategy goes fully long TQQQ; partial disagreement yields a 50/50 split.
overfit_notes: MFI period of 10 and threshold of 50 are both relatively standard; the 200-day median split (index 100 of sorted closes) is a slightly unusual trend gate but not aggressively tuned. Overfit risk is low.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit (MR)
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: MFI(14) >40 (loose)
description: A loosened variant of the MFI median trend-follower, using a below-neutral MFI threshold of 40 to allow entry under weaker money-flow conditions. This makes the strategy more permissive: it enters TQQQ whenever the price is above its median and even modest positive flow is present.
overfit_notes: MFI(14) threshold of 40 is deliberately loose and below the neutral 50 midline, reducing the strength filter's selectivity. The 50/50 mixed allocation is standard. One slightly tuned threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: CCI + Median
description: A trend-following strategy combining a 20-period Commodity Channel Index with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ. CCI above zero indicates bullish momentum; paired with the median trend gate, this drives a three-state TQQQ/BIL allocation.
overfit_notes: CCI(20) is a standard period; the threshold of zero (neutral line) rather than the conventional ±100 overbought/oversold levels is a slight but reasonable deviation. Two components, one lightly non-canonical threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: Tenkan/Kijun Cross + Median
description: A trend-following strategy using the Ichimoku Tenkan-sen (9-period midpoint) versus Kijun-sen (26-period midpoint) relationship as a momentum signal, combined with a 200-day price median filter. When Tenkan is above Kijun (a standard bullish Ichimoku signal) and the median gate passes, the strategy holds TQQQ.
overfit_notes: Tenkan (9) and Kijun (26) periods are the textbook Ichimoku defaults; the 200-day median is standard. All components at canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: Stochastic(50,5,5) + Median
description: A trend-following strategy using a slow, wide 50-period Stochastic oscillator above its midline (50) as a momentum filter, combined with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ. The extra-long Stochastic period dampens noise and provides a slower-turning momentum signal.
overfit_notes: Stochastic period of 50 with smoothing (5,5) is significantly non-standard versus the canonical (14,3,3); threshold of %K > 50 is the midline, which is standard. One notably non-canonical period choice.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: Up/Down Vol Ratio + Median
description: A trend-following strategy using an up/down volume ratio as a market breadth filter: summing volume on up-days versus down-days over the past 20 sessions and requiring buyers to dominate. Combined with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ, it drives a three-state TQQQ/BIL allocation.
overfit_notes: The 20-day lookback window and the ratio threshold of 1.0 are both round numbers and reasonable defaults. Two standard-feeling components, though the up/down volume ratio is less common than MFI.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Sum of volume on up-days / sum of volume on down-days (last 20 days) > 1.0

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: Range Expanded 110%
description: A volatility-expansion trend follower that enters TQQQ when QQQ's recent 25-day average range exceeds 110% of the 200-day average, indicating elevated volatility, combined with a median trend gate. This tests whether periods of rising volatility in an uptrend produce strong forward returns.
overfit_notes: The 110% threshold inverts the compression logic; it is one step in a family of threshold sweeps. Elevated volatility entry is a non-standard signal direction compared to breakout compression strategies. Moderate tuning.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 110% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: Range Expanded 120%
description: A variant of the range-expansion trend follower with a tighter expansion requirement: the recent 25-day average range must exceed 120% of the 200-day historical average. This fires less frequently than the 110% variant and targets more pronounced volatility expansions.
overfit_notes: The 120% threshold is a step in the same family of range-expansion threshold sweeps. The concept is the same as the 110% variant with one adjusted parameter.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 120% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

name: Dual Mom 20+60 4-state
description: A four-state momentum trend follower combining the 200-day price median with two separate lookback momentum measures (20-day and 60-day). All three votes drive a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation. This tests whether multi-timeframe momentum agreement (1-month and 3-month) improves timing.
overfit_notes: 20-day and 60-day momentum lookbacks are both standard timeframes; positive-return thresholds need no tuning. The 4-state allocation table is a parameterized design choice. Three components at near-canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 20-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 60-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

name: VIX-Fix Calm Entry + Median
description: A volatility-filtered trend follower using the Williams VIX-Fix (a synthetic volatility measure computed from price relative to a 22-day high) as a calm/stress indicator. Entry requires low synthetic VIX (below 3%) during an uptrend; exit triggers when the VIX-Fix spikes above 8% or the trend fails.
overfit_notes: The 22-day high window for VIX-Fix is a standard Williams parameter; the calm threshold of 3% and exit threshold of 8% are specific tuned values with no canonical basis. Two thresholds introducing moderate overfit risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: VIX-Fix > 8% OR trend gate fails → 100% BIL

name: Dual Mom 30+90 4-state
description: A four-state momentum trend follower using medium and longer-term 30-day and 90-day momentum lookbacks alongside the 200-day median filter. The three votes drive a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation, testing whether intermediate and quarterly momentum agreement improves timing.
overfit_notes: 30-day and 90-day momentum lookbacks are standard timeframes; positive-return thresholds need no tuning. The 4-state table is a design parameter. Three components at near-canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 30-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 90-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

name: Triple Mom 5-state
description: A five-state momentum trend follower that counts four independent positive-return signals: the 200-day median filter, 10-day, 30-day, and 90-day momentum. The count (0–4) maps to five graduated TQQQ/BIL allocations in 25% increments. This is the most complex pure-momentum variant in this series.
overfit_notes: Four components (median + three momentum periods), all at near-standard settings. The 5-state allocation table with 25% steps (1.0/0.75/0.5/0.25/0.0) is a parameterized design. The specific three momentum lookbacks (10/30/90) introduce mild selection overfit.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 10-day return > 0 (1 vote); 30-day return > 0 (1 vote); 90-day return > 0 (1 vote)

name: Mom20+Median+Top1
description: A hybrid trend follower that pairs the standard 20-day momentum and 200-day median timing signals with a universe selection component: the single largest-cap stock from the top 100 by dollar volume is used as the cash-alternative exposure in mixed states. When both signals are bullish, 100% goes to TQQQ; in mixed states, 50% goes to TQQQ and 50% to the top market-cap stock.
overfit_notes: The 20-day momentum and 200-day median are both standard. Using the top-1 market-cap stock as the non-leveraged allocation (instead of BIL) is a non-canonical design that benefits from large-cap bias in the backtest period. Mild overfit from this vehicle selection.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% top-cap stock; neither true → 100% top-cap stock

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 1 stock by market cap from top 100 by dollar volume

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest-cap stock

name: Dual Mom Top-1 Step-Down
description: A four-state hybrid that combines the 200-day median, 20-day momentum, and 60-day momentum to drive a step-down allocation across TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and BIL. As the vote count drops from 3 to 0, the strategy steps down from pure TQQQ through mixed TQQQ/top-cap, to pure top-cap, and finally to a 50/50 top-cap/BIL split.
overfit_notes: Three components (median + two momentum periods) at near-standard settings; the specific 4-state allocation plan mixing TQQQ, top-cap stock, and BIL in an asymmetric step-down is a highly specific design choice. The universe selection (top market cap stock) adds vehicle-selection risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 20-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 60-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 50% TQQQ / 50% top-cap; 1 vote → 100% top-cap; 0 votes → 50% top-cap / 50% BIL

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 1 stock by market cap from top 100 by dollar volume

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest-cap stock / BIL
